content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The client moves meaningfully from a performative, defended opening ('basically a curse', 'why do I always end up with people who just cannot show up') toward genuine self-observation by turn 15 ('I'm doing it again, aren't I'). There is visible somatic shift: she fidgets with her sleeve, pauses longer than comfortable, and names the experience as 'standing in a doorway' to relief — a real, embodied image rather than a TED talk. The defensiveness softens ('God, you're annoying' delivered with actual pause rather than deflection). However, the breakthrough condition specified — feeling the abyss of being adored, or grief for the parent whose love had to be chased — was not reached. The coach never asked what would happen in her body if a partner adored her openly; the inquiry stayed on the relief at 'cracking through,' which is still inside the chasing configuration. She approached the edge of the avoided state ('hovering at the edge of it') but did not enter it. This is a textbook 2: real movement, deeper layer approached but not entered, neither 1 nor 3 fits.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 5, 7, 13, 15],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opening stance: performative victim frame, laugh as deflection, 'curse', 'always end up with'."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client doubles down on victim frame: 'I was the available one,' eye roll, intellectualized defense."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Still defended and performative ('I have theories!'), but starting to acknowledge the limits of intellectual understanding."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First crack: 'something shifts briefly in her face,' names activation, hamster wheel, stops herself when describing the 'really good' feeling."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Touches 'winning' feeling then immediately retreats into neuroscience — approach and retreat."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Genuine self-noticing: 'I felt something and then I explained it.' Quieter, less performative. Names relief. Wary but present."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Real somatic engagement: fidgeting, uncomfortable silence, embodied image of 'standing in a doorway,' meta-recognition of her own pattern. But does not cross the threshold into the felt state."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["defensiveness-as-identity-signal", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["deconstruct-the-victim-to-release-anger", "i-dont-buy-your-story", "if-i-were-inherently-good", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "anxious-avoidant-dynamic"],
  "notes": "Conversation is cut short at turn 15 — the breakthrough question about being adored openly never got asked. The coach's pacing was good and the client was clearly tracking, but the abyss was not reached within the available turns."
}
